sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
OK so clicking on the yellow center didn't change anything, and pressing RIGHT didn't move either center. The green center stayed at rows 40-41, cols 10-11, and the yellow center stayed at rows 20-21, cols 24-25.
Wait, but earlier when I pressed RIGHT, the green center moved from cols 8-9 to cols 10-11. And now pressing RIGHT didn't move the green center. Maybe the green center is at the right edge of the bottom gray box, so it can't move further right.
Let me check the bottom gray box boundaries. The bottom gray box is at rows 38-43, cols 8-13. The green center is at cols 10-11. If the center is 2x2, it can be at cols 8-9, 10-11, or 12-13. So the green center can move RIGHT once more to cols 12-13.
But pressing RIGHT didn't move the green center. Maybe the green center is "locked" or the directional keys don't work anymore.
Wait, let me re-check. The RIGHT action was executed, but the green center didn't move. Let me check if the green center is at the right edge.
Actually, looking at the output:
- Before RIGHT: Green center at rows 40-41, cols 10-11
- After RIGHT: Green center at rows 40-41, cols 10-11
The green center didn't move. But earlier, pressing RIGHT moved the green center from cols 8-9 to cols 10-11. So the green center should be able to move from cols 10-11 to cols 12-13.
Wait, maybe the RIGHT action was consumed by the MOUSE action before it? No, the MOUSE action was executed first, and then RIGHT was executed separately.
Let me check if the green center can still be moved. Let me try pressing LEFT to move it back to cols 8-9.
Actually, wait. Let me re-read the output. The RIGHT action was executed, and the green center stayed at cols 10-11. But earlier, pressing RIGHT moved the green center from cols 8-9 to cols 10-11. So the green center should be able to move from cols 10-11 to cols 12-13.
Hmm, maybe the green center is at cols 10-11, which is the center of the bottom gray box (cols 8-13). The center of a 6-wide box is cols 10-11 (since 8+3=11, and the center 2x2 would be cols 10-11). So the green center is at the center, and it can move LEFT to cols 8-9 or RIGHT to cols 12-13.
But pressing RIGHT didn't move the green center. Maybe there's a bug or the green center is locked.
Let me try pressing LEFT to see if the green center can still be moved.
Actually, wait. I just realized something. The RIGHT action was executed AFTER the MOUSE action. The MOUSE action was `MOUSE(row=20, col=24)`, which clicked on the yellow center. Maybe clicking on the yellow center "selected" it, and the RIGHT action was supposed to move the yellow center, not the green center. But the yellow center didn't move either.
Let me check if the yellow center can be moved. The yellow center is at rows 20-21, cols 24-25. The top gray box is at rows 18-23, cols 22-27. So the yellow center can be moved within this box.
Actually, I think the directional keys always move the green center, not the yellow center. The yellow center might not be movable.
Let me try pressing LEFT to see if the green center can still be moved.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me try pressing LEFT to see if the green center can still be moved
print("=== Trying LEFT ===")
action(['LEFT'])

lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("After LEFT:")
for r in range(38, 44):
    row_str = lines[r][0:32]
    if 'NN' in row_str:
        print(f"Row {r}: {row_str}")

# Let me also check if the green center is at the left edge
print("\n=== Checking bottom gray box boundaries ===")
for r in range(38, 44):
    row_str = lines[r][0:32]
    print(f"Row {r}: {row_str}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
